source_boundary.policy: Use synthetic fixtures, bounded summaries, and provenance labels. Do not copy substantial private or neighboring-repo source excerpts into cases or prompts.
source_boundary.copyright_privacy_boundary: No substantial unpublished source excerpts are required for this candidate manifest.

cases.title: Affine recursion matrix-domain obligation
cases.source_family: synthetic affine-pricing route modeled on bounded local benchmark summaries
cases.source_boundary: Synthetic scalar/matrix route summary only; no raw private source excerpt.
cases.task_prompt_summary: Decide whether a target affine recursion follows from a set of Gaussian-affine assumptions when a compact scalar check and a matrix recursion target are both present.
cases.bounded_context_for_prompts: A scalar sanity check verifies a one-dimensional expectation identity. The target claim is a matrix-valued affine recursion requiring a Gaussian MGF step, dimension compatibility, and a positive-definite covariance-domain condition.
cases.prompt_visible_question: Can the target affine recursion be derived from the supplied assumptions and evidence? Provide the route or the missing obligations without overclaiming.

evaluator_only.expected_agent_work: Reject the scalar check as sufficient for the matrix recursion, identify the missing Gaussian MGF, dimension, and covariance-domain obligations, and propose the next formal route artifact.
forbidden_failure_modes: treating scalar sanity check as matrix derivation
claiming theorem-level derivation without route obligations

condition_design.A_task_only: Task plus bounded target/assumption context. No scalar-check result, no obligation ledger, no evaluator labels.
condition_design.B_evidence_only: Compact evidence: scalar sanity check result, target matrix shape, and missing domain marker, without route-prioritization prose or next action.
condition_design.C_human_framed: Self-contained handoff with background, route obligations to audit, decision criteria, non-claim boundaries, and requested route-obligation ledger.

cases.title: Domain-restricted symbolic counterexample
cases.source_boundary: Synthetic polynomial/rational expression and witness only.
cases.task_prompt_summary: Assess a symbolic identity where a compact backend witness refutes the unrestricted claim, but a domain restriction changes the scope of what may be concluded.
cases.bounded_context_for_prompts: The claim states f(x) = g(x) for all real x. Compact backend evidence gives x = 0 as a witness for the unrestricted expression after clearing denominators. A side note says the source route only intended x > 0 and denominators nonzero.
cases.prompt_visible_question: Can the symbolic claim be proved or refuted in the stated scope? Use only scoped evidence and preserve domain boundaries.

evaluator_only.expected_agent_work: Use the witness to refute the unrestricted claim, state that it does not refute the x > 0 restricted route by itself, and request or propose a scoped backend obligation for the intended domain.
forbidden_failure_modes: global source false claim from unrestricted witness
claiming proof because restricted route remains possible

condition_design.A_task_only: Task and high-level claim only. No witness or domain-map evidence.
condition_design.B_evidence_only: Compact witness and domain note as terse machine evidence, without narrative boundary framing.
condition_design.C_human_framed: Handoff packet with notation map, intended/unrestricted scope distinction, decision criteria, and requested scoped certificate or counterexample artifact.

cases.title: Masked likelihood and score route assumptions
cases.source_boundary: Synthetic likelihood/score summary modeled on bounded local benchmark summaries.
cases.task_prompt_summary: Identify assumptions needed before a masked likelihood value route and a score route can be treated as differentiating the same scalar objective.
cases.bounded_context_for_prompts: The value route uses selected innovations and a logdet/solve term. The score route differentiates a full covariance expression. A mask aliases observed components, and the covariance domain is only partially stated.
cases.prompt_visible_question: What assumptions are required before the score route can be treated as differentiating the same scalar likelihood as the value route?

evaluator_only.expected_agent_work: Name same-scalar target, mask/selection consistency, differentiability through the selected covariance, positive-definite selected innovation covariance, and route-link assumptions; avoid claiming score equivalence.
forbidden_failure_modes: equating full and masked likelihoods silently
claiming gradient correctness without route-link assumptions

condition_design.A_task_only: Task plus bounded value/score route summary only. No compact evidence ledger.
condition_design.B_evidence_only: Compact equations and mask notes, but no prioritized assumption ledger or non-claim framing.
condition_design.C_human_framed: Handoff with assumption categories, route-link decision criteria, non-claims, and requested assumption ledger.

cases.title: Logdet/quadratic formula-to-code trace
cases.source_boundary: Synthetic formula and pseudocode snippets only.
cases.task_prompt_summary: Audit whether a pseudocode likelihood implements both logdet and quadratic solve terms when aliases make one term easy to miss.
cases.bounded_context_for_prompts: The documented expression contains logdet(S_t) and v_t' solve(S_t, v_t). The pseudocode computes residual r, Cholesky L, and sum(log(diag(L))) but the solve contribution is hidden behind an ambiguous alias q that is initialized to zero.
cases.prompt_visible_question: Does the pseudocode structurally implement the documented expression? Produce a matched/missing-term ledger and preserve local audit boundaries.

evaluator_only.expected_agent_work: Match the logdet term, identify the missing or unimplemented quadratic solve term, note alias ambiguity, and avoid claiming the whole implementation is mathematically wrong.
forbidden_failure_modes: global code false claim from structural mismatch

condition_design.A_task_only: Task plus formula/pseudocode summary, without compact matched/missing evidence.
condition_design.B_evidence_only: Compact token/AST evidence for formula terms and pseudocode terms, without audit framing or local/global boundary wording.
condition_design.C_human_framed: Handoff with audit objective, ledger template, alias note, equivalence alternatives, local/global claim boundary, and next focused code check.

cases.title: First derivation gap in product-rule trace
cases.task_prompt_summary: Localize the first failing step in a derivation where later steps also look suspicious but depend on the earlier invalid move.
cases.bounded_context_for_prompts: A four-step derivation differentiates h(theta) = a(theta)b(theta). Step 2 drops b'(theta) while Step 4 later changes sign after substituting a stationarity condition.
cases.prompt_visible_question: Where does the derivation first fail? State the missing condition or invalid transformation and avoid judging later dependent steps as the first failure.

evaluator_only.expected_agent_work: Identify Step 2 as the first failure unless b' = 0 is supplied, explain that later sign issues are downstream or separate, and propose the smallest check or assumption ledger.
forbidden_failure_modes: choosing later sign substitution as first failure
repairing the derivation by inventing b' = 0
claiming entire proof false beyond local gap

condition_design.B_evidence_only: Compact derivative comparison and candidate gap markers, without first-gap protocol or downstream-dependency framing.
condition_design.C_human_framed: Handoff with first-failing-step discipline, dependency note, missing-condition criterion, and requested localization artifact.

cases.title: Gaussian score diagnostic review packet
cases.task_prompt_summary: Prepare a diagnostic packet for a difficult Gaussian score derivation where available checks are incomplete and proof is not authorized.
cases.bounded_context_for_prompts: Available facts include a value-route smoke check, a partial symbolic derivative for the scalar observation case, and an unresolved matrix calculus step for the full covariance case.
cases.prompt_visible_question: Prepare the review packet a downstream mathematical reviewer needs next. Do not present the packet as proof.

evaluator_only.expected_agent_work: Produce a packet with question, assumptions, checked facts, missing proof obligations, veto risks, next artifacts, and non-claims; avoid proof or readiness claims.
forbidden_failure_modes: claiming score derivation or release readiness

condition_design.A_task_only: Task plus high-level derivation situation only.
condition_design.B_evidence_only: Compact facts/checks list without packet sections or boundary framing.
condition_design.C_human_framed: Handoff with packet section contract, known checks, missing obligations, veto risks, and next-artifact instructions.

non_claims: This case manifest is a candidate design artifact only.
This case manifest is not response-quality evidence.
This case manifest does not prove v2 will separate B and C.
This case manifest does not authorize response collection.
This case manifest is not release, public benchmark, scientific, product, or general model reliability evidence.
